title: A1 - Psychology as a Science
purpose: Control map for rebuilding A1 as a true 10-day unit, not one compressed lesson package.
source_authority: Cycle A Teacher Binder v3 SOURCE_INTEGRATED: A1 ten-day teacher sequence
Cycle A Student Packet Bundle v3 SOURCE_INTEGRATED: A1 vocabulary organizer, handouts 1-10, final assessment, exit tickets
OpenStax Psychology 2e Chapters 1-2 as the default content spine
master_spec_controls: Generate classroom-usable materials, not textbook summaries.
Use one clear core idea per lesson.
Build around student thinking: classify, compare, apply, evaluate, explain, or revise.
Require decision plus reason in every major activity.
Use fictional or composite examples for sensitive topics.
Never ask students to diagnose themselves, classmates, family members, public figures, or fictional characters.
Generate three aligned tiers by default for major worksheets: Supported, Proficient, Extending.
Default lesson flow: Engage/Hook, Learn/Concept, Do/Experience, Make Sense/Guided Consolidation, Apply + Check.
unit_question: How do psychologists study behaviour responsibly?
safety_level: Low to moderate: keep examples fictional/general; no diagnosis, no personal mental health data, no live class research without approval.
teacher_marking_notes: Do not reward unsupported certainty, diagnosis, ranking, or one-cause explanations.
Credit students who accurately name uncertainty, limits, and context.
For supported versions, mark concept accuracy and evidence before polish.
For extending versions, expect comparison, critique, and transfer.

days.teacher_task: Students distinguish psychology from opinion, advice, therapy, personality quizzes, stereotypes, and research-based claims.
days.student_task: Sort claims into opinion, advice, stereotype, therapy/diagnosis, or researchable psychology. Rewrite one weak claim as a careful research question.
days.exit_ticket: What makes a psychology claim testable?

days.teacher_task: Students turn vague ideas into researchable questions and measurable variables.
days.student_task: Choose a vague idea such as stress, confidence, focus, friendship, memory, or motivation. Name possible variables. Write an operational definition for how each could be measured.

days.teacher_focus: Research methods: what each method can show
days.teacher_task: Students compare survey, case study, naturalistic observation, and experiment.
days.student_task: Match research questions to survey, case study, naturalistic observation, or experiment. Explain why the method fits. Name one limitation for each choice.
days.exit_ticket: Which method fits your question and why?

days.teacher_task: Students explain why a relationship between variables does not prove cause.
days.student_task: For each correlation, write at least two alternative explanations. Identify possible direction problems and third variables. Use the frame: This shows a relationship, but it does not prove...
days.exit_ticket: Why does correlation not prove causation?

days.teacher_task: Students identify ethical concerns and propose safer research choices.
days.student_task: Identify consent, privacy, harm, deception, debriefing, and right-to-withdraw concerns. Revise fictional study ideas to make them safer. Explain why interesting research can still be unethical.

days.teacher_task: Students read a short source-based excerpt and extract method, evidence, limits, and ethical concerns.
days.student_handout: Handout 6 - Reading: How Psychologists Study Behaviour
days.student_task: Underline the research method. Circle a limitation. Star an ethical concern. Answer: what does the evidence show and not show?
days.exit_ticket: What did the reading prove/not prove?

days.teacher_task: Students evaluate weak psychology-style claims using a repeated reasoning frame.
days.student_task: Choose a weak psychology-style claim. Identify the claim, evidence needed, best method, alternative explanation, ethics, and careful rewrite.

days.teacher_task: Students design a simple ethical hypothetical study to test a question.
days.student_task: Write a research question, hypothesis, variables, method, sample, limit, and ethical protections. Peer-check for clarity, measurability, and ethical caution.

days.teacher_task: Students review the final task, rubric, and sample responses.
days.student_handout: Handout 9 - Assessment Prep: Model Response Sort
days.student_task: Compare emerging, proficient, and extending sample responses. Highlight concept use, evidence, limits, and ethics. Plan your own final response.

days.teacher_task: Students independently evaluate a psychology-style claim.
days.student_handout: Handout 10 - Final Assessment Planning Sheet and A1 Final Assessment - Flawed Claim Detective
days.student_task: Select or receive a flawed claim. Plan evidence needed, method, alternative explanation, ethical issue, and careful conclusion.

build_rule: Create ten renderable daily packages for A1. Each day must produce its own teacher guide PDF, student sheet PDF with Supported/Proficient/Extending tasks, slide deck PPTX, and exit ticket PDF. Do not replace this with one compressed unit packet.
